attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
2	import { runScenario } from "./claim-office.js";
3	
4	describe("MHPCO Claim Office", () => {
5	  // ===== QUOTE: single main item, first insurance, no risk modifiers =====
6	  // Base premium + 10% first-insurance surcharge + 5 G fee
7	  it("quote: single sword for new customer (first insurance) — premium 115 G (100 base + 10% surcharge = 110, +5 fee)", () => {
8	    const result = runScenario({
9	      customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: 0 },
10	      steps: [
11	        {
12	          op: "quote",
13	          items: [
14	            { type: "sword", material: "steel", enchantment: 0, cursed: false },
15	          ],
16	        },
17	      ],
18	    });
19	    expect(result).toEqual({ results: [{ premium: 115 }] });
20	  });
21	  it.todo(
22	    "quote: single amulet for new customer (first insurance) — premium 71 G (60 base + 10% = 66, +5 fee)"
23	  );
24	  it.todo(
25	    "quote: single staff for new customer (first insurance) — premium 93 G (80 base + 10% = 88, +5 fee)"
26	  );
27	  it.todo(
28	    "quote: single potion for new customer (first insurance) — premium 49 G (40 base + 10% = 44, +5 fee)"
29	  );
30	
31	  // ===== QUOTE: single component, first insurance =====
32	  it.todo(
33	    "quote: single rune for new customer — premium 33 G (25 base + 10% = 27.5 -> 28, +5 fee)"
34	  );
35	  it.todo(
36	    "quote: single moonstone for new customer — premium 33 G (25 base + 10% = 27.5 -> 28, +5 fee)"
37	  );
38	
39	  // ===== QUOTE: building block of 3 alike components =====
40	  it.todo(
41	    "quote: 3 runes (building block) for new customer — premium 71 G (60 block base + 10% = 66, +5 fee)"
42	  );
43	  it.todo(
44	    "quote: 3 moonstones (building block) for new customer — premium 71 G (60 block base + 10% = 66, +5 fee)"
45	  );
46	  it.todo(
47	    "quote: 2 runes (not enough for block) for new customer — premium 60 G (2 × 25 = 50 base, +10% = 55, +5 fee)"
48	  );
49	  it.todo(
50	    "quote: 4 runes (one block of 3 + 1 leftover) for new customer — premium 99 G (60 block + 25 leftover = 85, +10% = 93.5 -> 94, +5 fee)"
51	  );
52	  it.todo(
53	    "quote: 3 mixed components (2 runes + 1 moonstone) — no block applies, premium 88 G (3 × 25 = 75, +10% = 82.5 -> 83, +5 fee)"
54	  );
55	
56	  // ===== QUOTE: cursed item surcharge (+50%) =====
57	  it.todo(
58	    "quote: cursed sword for new customer — premium 170 G (100 base × 1.5 cursed = 150, × 1.1 first = 165, +5 fee)"
59	  );
60	
61	  // ===== QUOTE: highly enchanted item (enchantment >= 5) surcharge (+30%) =====
62	  it.todo(
63	    "quote: sword with enchantment 5 for new customer — premium 148 G (100 × 1.3 = 130, × 1.1 = 143, +5 fee)"
64	  );
65	  it.todo(
66	    "quote: sword with enchantment 4 (below threshold) for new customer — premium 115 G (100 × 1.1 = 110, +5 fee)"
67	  );
68	
69	  // ===== QUOTE: cursed AND highly enchanted (both surcharges apply) =====
70	  it.todo(
71	    "quote: cursed sword with enchantment 7 for new customer — premium 220 G (100 × 1.5 × 1.3 = 195, × 1.1 = 214.5 -> 215, +5 fee)"
72	  );
73	
74	  // ===== QUOTE: long-standing customer (yearsWithMHPCO >= 2) loyalty discount (-20%) =====
75	  it.todo(
76	    "quote: single sword for loyal customer (2 years), first insurance — premium 93 G (100 × 0.8 = 80, × 1.1 = 88, +5 fee)"
77	  );
78	  it.todo(
79	    "quote: single sword for customer with 1 year (below loyalty threshold) — premium 115 G (no loyalty discount)"
80	  );
81	
82	  // ===== QUOTE: subsequent contract discount (-15% each after the first) =====
83	  it.todo(
84	    "quote: second quote for new customer gets -15% (no first-insurance surcharge) — second premium 90 G (100 × 0.85 = 85, +5 fee)"
85	  );
86	  it.todo(
87	    "quote: third quote for new customer also gets -15% — third premium 90 G"
88	  );
89	
90	  // ===== QUOTE: multiple main items in one quote =====
91	  it.todo(
92	    "quote: sword + amulet for new customer — premium 181 G (100+60 = 160, × 1.1 = 176, +5 fee)"
93	  );
94	
95	  // ===== QUOTE: combined modifiers - loyal customer + first insurance =====
96	  it.todo(

98	  );
99	
100	  // ===== QUOTE: combined modifiers - loyal + cursed + enchanted on second contract =====
101	  it.todo(
102	    "quote: loyal customer's second quote with cursed enchanted sword — applies all modifiers (cursed × enchanted × loyalty × subsequent + fee)"
103	  );
104	
105	  // ===== QUOTE: rounding in MHPCO's favor (round up) =====
106	  it.todo(
107	    "quote: verify rounding goes up (in MHPCO's favor) when premium would have a fractional G"
108	  );
109	
110	  // ===== CLAIM: enchantment >= 8 reimbursed at 50% =====
111	  it.todo(
112	    "claim: damage to item with enchantment 8, amount 400 G — payout 100 G (50% of 400 = 200, -100 deductible)"
113	  );
114	  it.todo(

116	  );
117	
118	  // ===== CLAIM: dragon material fully reimbursed =====
119	  it.todo(

121	  );
122	
123	  // ===== CLAIM: multiple damages share one deductible per incident =====
124	  it.todo(

126	  );
127

131	  );
132	
133	  // ===== INTEGRATION: quote then claim referencing earlier policy =====
134	  it.todo(
135	    "scenario: quote followed by claim against policy index 0 returns both premium and payout in results"
136	  );
137

139	  it.todo(

142	});
